steps.title: Evaluate each choice against the documented flaw
steps.content:
• Choice A — exaggerate the effect of increasing human activity on small mammals.: The passage says large-forest errors apply to large-mammal predictions, not small mammals. ✗
  • Choice B — overestimate the actual population sizes of large mammals.: Beaverhead-Deerlodge is a large forest, and the model's predictions for large mammals in large forests are "too high" — meaning it overestimates those populations. ✓
  • Choice C — ignore the predator-prey relationships between large and small mammals.: Predator-prey relationships are never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — reflect factors affecting large mammals other than increasing human activity.: The passage describes the error as predictions being too high, not as the model picking up irrelevant variables. ✗

steps.title: Apply the rule to Beaverhead-Deerlodge
steps.content: The passage states one clear rule: in large forests, large-mammal predictions are too high. Beaverhead-Deerlodge is a large forest, so the model would overestimate large-mammal population sizes. That matches Choice B — overestimate the actual population sizes of large mammals..
